product_overview: A single-page, host-operated quiz dashboard for running live events (Q-Mania Club). The app prioritizes manual control: hosts create teams, draw questions, control attempts and scoring, and view a live leaderboard in real time via Socket.IO.
core_goals: Provide a single-page host console that gives full manual control over quiz flow and scoring.
Allow hosts to create and validate teams, draw random non-repeating questions, and run two-attempt question flows.
Show a live, projector-friendly leaderboard that updates in real time and highlights the active team.
Include robust undo, reset, and manual-adjustment capabilities to recover from mistakes and handle edge cases.
Keep the system simple, reliable, and fast with minimal automation and explicit host-triggered actions.

features.name: Team Setup
features.description: Host creates teams, validates names, sets initial scores to zero, and launches the game.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Add team rows up to maximum team limit -> Enter unique non-empty team names into each row -> Click Launch Game -> Observe teams listed with score = 0
Navigate to / -> Add team rows up to maximum team limit -> Leave one team name empty -> Click Launch Game -> See validation error 'Team name cannot be empty' and prevent launch
Navigate to / -> Add team rows up to maximum team limit -> Enter duplicate team names across rows -> Click Launch Game -> See validation error 'Team names must be unique' and prevent launch
Navigate to / -> Click Add team rows beyond allowed maximum -> UI prevents additional rows and shows 'Maximum teams reached' message

features.name: Live Quiz Host Control
features.description: Host selects sections, draws the next random question, reveals answers/hints, runs Attempt 1 and Attempt 2 for teams, and records scoring actions manually.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Select a section from section selector -> Click 'Next Random Question' -> Observe question displayed in center panel -> Click 'Reveal Answer' -> Observe answer and hints displayed -> Select a team from Team Control -> Click 'Start Attempt 1' -> Click 'Correct' -> Observe +section score applied to selected team -> Click 'End Question' -> Observe leaderboard update

Navigate to / -> Select a section -> Click 'Next Random Question' -> Observe question displayed -> Select a team -> Click 'Start Attempt 1' -> Click 'Wrong' -> Observe -section penalty applied -> Click 'Start Attempt 2' -> Select another team -> Click 'Correct' -> Observe scoring applied -> Click 'End Question'

Navigate to / -> Select a section -> Click 'Next Random Question' repeatedly until questions exhausted -> Click 'Next Random Question' -> See message 'No questions remaining for this section' and Next button disabled

Navigate to / -> Select a section -> Click 'Next Random Question' -> Observe question displayed -> Click 'Next Random Question' again immediately -> System draws new question and ensures new question ID != previous question ID; if duplicate would occur show warning 'Duplicate question prevented'

features.name: Dynamic Leaderboard and End-Game Results
features.description: Live sorted leaderboard updates during gameplay; host can end the game to see final standings and warnings for teams that did not attempt required sections.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> During scoring, perform a scoring action (Correct or Wrong) -> Observe leaderboard update in real time, sorted by score and highlighting active team
Navigate to / -> After multiple questions, click 'End Game' -> Observe final standings displayed and a warning list showing which teams did NOT attempt Section 1 and Section 2
Navigate to / -> Click 'End Game' when game state empty or no teams defined -> See validation 'Cannot end game: no teams configured' and prevent end

features.name: Question Management (Random, Non-Repeating)
features.description: Questions are stored on the backend and fetched randomly on host request; the UI prevents repeats and lets the host reveal answers and hints manually.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Select a section -> Click 'Next Random Question' -> Client emits socket event question:next -> Observe a random question displayed with question ID -> Click 'Reveal Answer' -> Observe answer and hints displayed
Navigate to / -> Draw questions repeatedly for a section until pool exhausted -> Click 'Next Random Question' -> Observe 'No questions remaining for this section' notice and disabled Next button

Navigate to / -> Click 'Next Random Question' -> Receive a question -> Click 'Next Random Question' again -> Backend returns a different question ID -> If backend would return a duplicate the client displays 'Duplicate question prevented' and host can re-request

features.name: Control Panel & Flow Control
features.description: A dedicated control panel exposes team selection, scoring buttons (correct/wrong or +/- values), attempt controls, and utility actions (skip/end/reset/next). Everything is manual and explicit.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Use Team Control dropdown to Select Team -> Click 'Start Attempt 1' -> Click 'Correct' -> Observe section-appropriate points added (+40/+60) -> Click 'End Question' -> Observe question lifecycle completed

Navigate to / -> Select Team -> Click 'Start Attempt 1' -> Click 'Wrong' -> Observe section-appropriate penalty applied (-10/-20) -> Click 'Start Attempt 2' -> Select next Team -> Click 'Correct' -> Observe points added -> Click 'End Question'

Navigate to / -> Select Team -> Click 'Correct' twice by mistake -> System prevents duplicate scoring for same attempt and shows 'Already scored for this attempt' warning -> Host clicks 'Undo Last Action' to revert the accidental second score
Navigate to / -> While a question is active click 'Skip Question' -> Observe question marked skipped and control returns to section's question count without applying scores

features.name: Manual Adjustments, Undo & Error Handling
features.description: Hosts can apply manual score adjustments, undo the last scoring action, reset the current question, and recover from accidental double scoring. System provides clear messages when actions cannot be performed.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> After a scoring action, click 'Undo Last Action' -> Observe last scoring action reverted and leaderboard updated accordingly
Navigate to / -> With no prior scoring actions, click 'Undo Last Action' -> See message 'No actions to undo' and no state change

Navigate to / -> After a scoring mistake, open manual score input -> Enter manual adjustment value and apply -> Observe leaderboard updated with manual adjustment -> Click 'Undo Last Action' -> Observe manual adjustment reverted

features.name: End-of-Game Reporting & Warnings
features.description: At game end the host sees final rankings and a mandatory display of which teams did not attempt Section 1 and Section 2 (informational only; no automatic penalties).
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Click 'End Game' -> Observe final sorted standings and a displayed list of teams that did NOT attempt Section 1 and Section 2 (warning only)
Navigate to / -> Click 'End Game' before any questions in Section 1 or 2 were attempted -> Observe warnings listing all teams for the relevant sections and 'No auto-penalty applied' note

routes.description: Single-page host console for quiz setup, live game control, and end-game leaderboard.

features.name: Team Setup
features.description: Host creates teams, validates names, and starts the game.
user_interactions: Add team rows up to maximum team limit.

features.name: Live Quiz Host Control
features.description: Host selects sections, draws questions, runs attempts, and scores teams in real time.
user_interactions: Select a section and draw next question.
Start attempt 1 or attempt 2 for a team.
Mark correct or wrong score and apply manual adjustments.
Skip, end, reset question, and undo last scoring action.

features.name: Dynamic Leaderboard and End-Game Results
features.description: Displays live ranking during gameplay and final standings at game end.
user_interactions: View live leaderboard updates while scoring.
End game and see final standings.
Review section-attempt warnings and reset game.

known_limitations.issue: No authentication/authorization layer; host controls are open to any connected client.
known_limitations.impact: Any connected user can trigger gameplay mutations.

known_limitations.issue: App is a single-page host workflow without dedicated route segmentation.
known_limitations.impact: Navigation/state testing must be phase-based rather than route-based.
